items.briefing:
Australians are being warned about a rise in deepfake-powered scams, with Chinese-developed software increasingly being used to create convincing fake videos and audio to deceive victims. These scams can impersonate colleagues, executives, or government officials, making them difficult to spot. Separately, cybercriminals have been caught hiding stolen data inside legitimate software packages on the RubyGems platform — a reminder that even trusted development tools can be compromised.

If your organisation uses Windows Server with Droplet backups, check that your backups are completing successfully, as a recent warning flags that legacy configurations may be silently failing.

**Practical tip:** Verify any unexpected video or voice requests for money or sensitive information by calling the person back on a known number.
